senses.definition: used before countable or singular nouns referring to people or things that have not already been mentioned
examples: I can only carry two at a time.
She's a friend of my father's (= one of my father's friends).

senses.definition: used to show that somebody/something is a member of a group or profession
examples: Is that a Monet (= a painting by Monet)?
A lion is a dangerous animal.

senses.definition: used before uncountable nouns when these have an adjective in front of them, or phrase following them
examples: a sadness that won’t go away

senses.definition: used in front of two nouns that are seen as a single unit
used instead of one before some numbers
used when talking about prices, quantities and rates
examples: I can type 50 words a minute.
He was driving at 50 miles an hour.

senses.definition: used before somebody’s name to show that the speaker does not know the person
examples: There's a Mrs Green to see you.

senses.definition: used before the names of days of the week to talk about one particular day
